notes: Comprehensive demo suite covering ALL agents, memory tiers, infrastructure components, and data tables.

Grounded in live NeonDB data: fraud_patterns (7), compliance_documents (5), fraud_cases (1000), policy_rules (6740, brands: visa/mastercard/unknown), policy_documents (3440), episodic_memory (8), analysis_decisions (8).
merchant_risk_profiles and learned_fraud_patterns are initially empty — populated during test runs.
Each test case lists which components it exercises and which NeonDB tables are queried by agents during execution.

component_coverage_legend.fraud_detection_agent: Agentic RAG with fraud_patterns + fraud_cases vector search
component_coverage_legend.compliance_agent: Agentic RAG with compliance_documents vector search
component_coverage_legend.risk_scoring_agent: Weighted score: 40% fraud, 30% compliance, 15% velocity, 15% historical
component_coverage_legend.investigation_agent: Deep-dive with episodic_memory recall (triggered when risk_score > 60)
component_coverage_legend.orchestrator: LangGraph state machine routing all agents
component_coverage_legend.kafka_events: 8 topics: lifecycle, detection, compliance, risk, investigation, coordination, memory
component_coverage_legend.episodic_memory: NeonDB: episodic_memory table with 1536-dim vector embeddings
component_coverage_legend.policy_data: NeonDB: policy_rules (6740) + policy_documents (3440) for Visa/MC rules
component_coverage_legend.agentic_rag_tools: 7 tools: search_similar_transactions, search_compliance_documents, search_fraud_patterns, recall_past_investigations, get_merchant_history, check_velocity, evaluate_retrieval_sufficiency

redis_short_term.description: Validate Redis session lifecycle during orchestration
checks: After /v1/analyze: stm:agent:{session_id}:fraud_detection is created
After /v1/analyze: stm:agent:{session_id}:compliance is created
After /v1/analyze: stm:agent:{session_id}:risk_scoring is created
After aggregation: all stm:session:* and stm:agent:* keys for that session are deleted
Velocity keys stm:velocity:merchant:{merchant_id} persist beyond session

redis_velocity.description: Validate velocity tracking in Redis sorted sets
checks: stm:velocity:merchant:{merchant_id}: sorted set with timestamp scores (60-min window)
stm:velocity:bin:{card_bin}: sorted set with timestamp scores (5-min window)
check_velocity() tool returns transaction count + HIGH/Normal status
Rapid same-merchant requests increment velocity count

kafka_events.description: Validate Kafka event publishing order and correlation
checks: All events share same correlation_id for one transaction
Events published to correct topics (fraud.analysis.lifecycle, fraud.detection.results, etc.)
Event payloads contain merchant_id, transaction_id, session_id

long_term_memory.description: Validate NeonDB long-term storage after orchestration
checks: analysis_decisions row count increases after each /v1/analyze call
New row contains: decision_id, merchant_id, transaction_ids, fraud_type, risk_score, decision
Feedback via /v1/feedback updates was_correct and feedback_notes columns
merchant_risk_profiles gets upsert for merchant after first analysis

episodic_memory.description: Validate episodic memory recording and recall
checks: After each analysis: new row in episodic_memory with 1536-dim embedding
Metadata includes: merchant_id, fraud_type, outcome, agents_involved, transaction_count
recall_past_investigations() tool returns semantically similar past episodes
Investigation agent uses recall to find precedent cases

policy_data.description: Validate policy tables availability for compliance/future policy agent
checks: policy_rules: 6740 rows, brands: visa, mastercard, unknown
policy_documents: 3440 rows from Visa/MC PDF source files
Compliance agent's search_compliance_documents() can retrieve from these tables
Data available for future dedicated policy agent node in orchestrator

test_cases.title: System health — all infrastructure connected
test_cases.description: Validates NeonDB, Redis, and Kafka connectivity

test_cases.title: Legitimate grocery transaction — low risk baseline
test_cases.description: In-store chip payment at a grocery store. Expects low risk score, no investigation. Validates all 3 core agents run, Redis session is created and cleaned, Kafka lifecycle events are published, and analysis_decisions gets a new row.
tables_queried_by_agents: compliance_documents (Visa VDMP/VFMP rules for visa card)

body_assertions: summary is a coherent prose sentence (not raw markdown bullets)

test_cases.title: Card testing pattern — micro CNP with repeated BIN
test_cases.description: Sub-dollar CNP ecommerce transaction at a telecom service. Matches pattern-card-testing-001 indicators: small amounts, rapid succession potential, same BIN. Fraud agent should search fraud_patterns and fraud_cases for card_testing matches.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search for similar card testing cases)

body_assertions: evidence references card testing indicators or micro amounts

test_cases.title: BIN attack — automated sequential card testing
test_cases.description: Micro-dollar CNP transaction at gaming merchant with a common BIN. Matches pattern-bin-attack-001 indicators: sequential_pans, micro_amounts, automated_speed. Also triggers BIN velocity tracking in Redis (stm:velocity:bin:543210).
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search for BIN attack cases)

body_assertions: risk analysis considers BIN pattern and micro amount
if risk_score <= 60: no investigation (but BIN patterns noted)

test_cases.redis_velocity_note: Check stm:velocity:bin:543210 and stm:velocity:merchant:m-digital-888 after execution

test_cases.title: Transaction laundering — MCC mismatch with international shipping
test_cases.description: High-value CNP manual-keyed transaction at a gift shop with billing/shipping country mismatch (US/GB). Matches pattern-transaction-laundering-001 indicators: mcc_mismatch, international_cards, website_mismatch. Compliance agent checks Mastercard BRAM for high-risk merchant indicators.
tables_queried_by_agents: policy_rules (Mastercard rules related to merchant risk assessment)

body_assertions: fraud_type is NEVER 'card_testing' (post-fix: fallback to UNKNOWN)
compliance analysis discusses BRAM or Mastercard merchant risk requirements
evidence references country mismatch (US/GB) or manual_keyed entry
risk_level in ['medium','high','critical','severe'] for strong signal cases
summary is coherent prose (not markdown bullets)

test_cases.title: Velocity abuse — sudden volume spike on a single merchant
test_cases.description: Multiple rapid transactions at a fuel station. Matches pattern-velocity-abuse-001 indicators: volume_spike, off_hours, identical_amounts. The check_velocity() RAG tool queries Redis stm:velocity:merchant:{merchant_id} sorted set.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search for velocity abuse patterns)

body_assertions: evidence mentions velocity or transaction frequency

test_cases.demo_tip: Run this test case 3-4 times rapidly to see velocity count increase in Redis stm:velocity:merchant:m-fuel-550. The check_velocity() tool will report elevated counts on subsequent runs.

test_cases.title: Visa VDMP compliance — dispute monitoring for supplement merchant
test_cases.description: CNP ecommerce at a supplement e-store (high-dispute MCC 5499) with cross-border shipping (US→CA). Compliance agent searches compliance-visa-vdmp-001 and compliance-visa-vfmp-001 documents for Visa dispute thresholds (0.9% ratio, 100 disputes/month).

body_assertions: compliance analysis references Visa VDMP/VFMP thresholds or dispute monitoring
risk_score reflects compliance signal (not just fraud)

test_cases.title: Mastercard ECM/BRAM — recurring subscription chargeback monitoring
test_cases.description: Recurring card-on-file payment at a streaming service. Compliance agent searches compliance-mc-ecm-001 (ECM: 100 chargebacks + 1.5% ratio thresholds) and compliance-mc-bram-001 (BRAM: high-risk MCC due diligence).

body_assertions: compliance analysis references ECM thresholds or BRAM requirements

test_cases.title: Synthetic identity — new customer, high-value electronics
test_cases.description: First-time customer purchasing high-value electronics. Matches pattern-synthetic-identity-001 indicators: thin_credit_file, rapid_account_creation, bust_out. Fraud_cases DB has confirmed synthetic_identity cases for vector comparison.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search for synthetic identity cases in DB)

body_assertions: fraud analysis considers synthetic identity or high-value first purchase

test_cases.title: Friendly fraud — recurring digital subscription dispute risk
test_cases.description: Recurring card-on-file at a gaming platform. Matches pattern-friendly-fraud-001 indicators: repeat_chargebacks, digital_goods, subscription. Fraud_cases DB contains confirmed friendly_fraud cases with family_fraud subtype.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search for friendly fraud cases, incl. family_fraud subtype)
compliance_documents (Visa VDMP for dispute monitoring on digital goods)

body_assertions: recommendations include monitoring or review actions

test_cases.title: Cross-merchant collusion — coordinated network detection
test_cases.description: CNP transaction from a suspicious 'catch-all' merchant (MCC 5999). Matches pattern-cross-merchant-collusion-001 indicators: shared_ownership, circular_transactions, common_ip, coordinated_timing. High-risk scenario likely to trigger investigation agent.
tables_queried_by_agents: compliance_documents (Mastercard BRAM for merchant network risk)

body_assertions: if risk_score > 60: investigation agent runs deep-dive analysis
evidence references network or merchant relationship concerns

test_cases.title: Account takeover — high-value airline ticket with SIM swap indicators
test_cases.description: High-value airline ticket purchase matching fraud_cases with account_takeover type (credential_stuffing subtype). Fraud_cases DB contains confirmed account_takeover cases in MCC 4511 (Airlines) with high risk scores.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_cases (vector search matches account_takeover cases: MCC 4511, high amounts, credential_stuffing)
compliance_documents (Visa VFMP for fraud monitoring on high-value CNP)

body_assertions: high value CNP with country mismatch elevates risk
evidence references high amount, CNP, or cross-border elements

test_cases.title: Amex OptBlue compliance — high-value travel merchant
test_cases.description: High-value CNP at a travel agency with Amex card. Compliance agent searches compliance-amex-optblue-001 for OptBlue program rules (1% chargeback ratio, $1M volume transition threshold).

body_assertions: compliance analysis references Amex OptBlue program or Amex-specific rules
response schema remains consistent (all fields present)

test_cases.title: High-risk investigation trigger — escalation deep-dive
test_cases.description: Deliberately crafted high-risk scenario: large CNP transfer at a gambling-adjacent merchant (MCC 7995) with country mismatches and suspicious IP. Designed to trigger risk_score > 60 and activate investigation_agent. Investigation agent uses recall_past_investigations to find precedent cases in episodic_memory.
tables_queried_by_agents: fraud_patterns (multiple pattern matches: velocity, laundering, BIN)
fraud_cases (vector search for high-risk transaction patterns)
compliance_documents (BRAM for MCC 7995 gambling, ECM thresholds)
episodic_memory (investigation_agent: recall_past_investigations for similar high-risk cases)
analysis_decisions (new row with 'escalated' or high-risk decision)

body_assertions: if risk_score > 60: summary mentions investigation findings or deep-dive
evidence should be multi-factor (amount, country mismatch, MCC risk, CNP)

test_cases.title: Batch analysis — mixed risk transaction batch
test_cases.description: Submit 2 transactions in a single batch: one low-risk grocery and one suspicious gaming micro-transaction. Validates batch endpoint processes all transactions and returns aggregated results.

body_assertions: each alert has: alert_id, fraud_type, risk_level, risk_score, evidence, recommendations

test_cases.title: Feedback loop — update analysis decision in long-term memory
test_cases.description: Submit feedback on a previous analysis decision. Updates was_correct and feedback_notes columns in analysis_decisions table (NeonDB long-term memory). Validates the feedback loop that improves future analysis accuracy.
test_cases.precondition: Run TC-002 (or any /v1/analyze case) first; capture the alert_id from the response and use it as decision_id here

json.feedback_notes: Confirmed legitimate transaction by manual review for demo run

expected.sql_validation: SELECT decision_id, was_correct, feedback_notes, feedback_at FROM analysis_decisions WHERE decision_id = '<alert_id>'

test_cases.title: Redis session cleanup — verify short-term memory lifecycle
test_cases.description: Run an analysis and then validate Redis key lifecycle: session keys (stm:session:*, stm:agent:*) are created during processing and deleted after aggregation, while velocity keys (stm:velocity:*) persist.

manual_validation: DURING processing: Redis keys stm:agent:{session_id}:fraud_detection, stm:agent:{session_id}:compliance, stm:agent:{session_id}:risk_scoring exist
AFTER completion: all stm:session:{session_id}:* and stm:agent:{session_id}:* keys are deleted
PERSISTS: stm:velocity:merchant:m-cleanup-01 sorted set remains with transaction timestamp
Redis CLI: KEYS stm:velocity:merchant:m-cleanup-01 → should return the key
Redis CLI: ZCARD stm:velocity:merchant:m-cleanup-01 → count of velocity events

test_cases.title: Merchant risk profile — empty table returns 404
test_cases.description: Query risk profile for a merchant when merchant_risk_profiles table is empty. Validates proper 404 error handling. After running TC-002 or TC-014, the table may have entries — then this validates profile data retrieval.

test_cases.title: Merchant risk profile — retrieve after analysis populates data
test_cases.description: After running TC-002 or other analysis cases, merchant_risk_profiles should have data for analyzed merchants. Validates that orchestrator's record_investigation_complete() upserts merchant profile to long-term memory.
test_cases.precondition: Run TC-002 first to populate merchant_risk_profiles for m-grocery-101

expected.note: If 404, run TC-002 first to populate merchant profile

test_cases.title: High-risk merchant listing endpoint
test_cases.description: Retrieve all merchants flagged as high-risk. Returns array of merchant profiles where is_high_risk=true. May be empty initially.

body_assertions: each item has: merchant_id, merchant_name, mcc, is_high_risk=true
may be empty if no high-risk merchants have been analyzed yet

test_cases.title: Policy data availability — Visa and Mastercard rules
test_cases.description: Validates that policy_rules (6740 rows across visa/mastercard/unknown brands) and policy_documents (3440 rows from PDF sources) exist in NeonDB. These tables are used by compliance_agent's search_compliance_documents() tool and will be consumed by a future dedicated policy agent.

request.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS policy_rules_count, COUNT(DISTINCT brand) AS brands FROM policy_rules

additional_sql_checks: SELECT DISTINCT source FROM policy_documents → includes Visa dispute resolution PDF
SELECT DISTINCT brand FROM policy_rules → visa, mastercard, unknown

test_cases.title: Episodic memory retrieval — semantic recall of past investigations
test_cases.description: After running several analysis cases, episodic_memory table contains investigation episodes. This validates that the recall_past_investigations() RAG tool can semantically retrieve relevant past episodes. Run multiple analysis TCs first to populate episodic memory, then run TC-014 (investigation trigger) — the investigation agent should reference similar past episodes.
test_cases.precondition: Run TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-009 first to populate episodic_memory with diverse episodes
tables_queried_by_agents: metadata filter by fraud_type, merchant_id, outcome
test_cases.validation_approach: Run TC-014 (high-risk investigation) after populating episodic memory. Investigation agent's summary should reference similar past cases from recall_past_investigations().

manual_validation: After TC-014: check analysis summary for references to past episodes
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM episodic_memory → should increase after each analysis
SQL: SELECT id, metadata->>'fraud_type', metadata->>'merchant_id', metadata->>'outcome' FROM episodic_memory ORDER BY metadata->>'timestamp' DESC LIMIT 10

test_cases.title: Kafka event correlation — verify event sequence and correlation_id
test_cases.description: Run an analysis and verify all Kafka events share the same correlation_id, are published in correct order, and contain expected payloads.
test_cases.precondition: Confluent Cloud topic viewer or local Kafka consumer active

manual_validation: Kafka topic fraud.analysis.lifecycle: analysis.started + analysis.completed events with same correlation_id
Kafka topic fraud.detection.results: fraud.detected or fraud.cleared event
Kafka topic fraud.compliance.results: compliance.check.passed or compliance.violation.found event
Kafka topic fraud.risk.scores: risk.score.calculated event with risk_level payload
All events have same correlation_id (UUID format)
Event payloads contain: merchant_id, transaction_id, session_id

test_cases.title: Rapid velocity test — multiple requests to trigger velocity detection
test_cases.description: Submit the same merchant transaction 3 times rapidly to trigger velocity detection in Redis. The check_velocity() RAG tool queries stm:velocity:merchant:{merchant_id} sorted set. On 3rd request, velocity count should be elevated, potentially increasing risk score.
execution_steps: Step 1: Send request below → note risk_score as baseline
Step 2: Send same request with transaction_id txn-demo-0016b → risk_score may increase
Step 3: Send same request with transaction_id txn-demo-0016c → risk_score should reflect elevated velocity

body_assertions: Risk score may increase across runs as velocity factor grows
evidence may reference velocity or transaction frequency on later runs
redis_validation: Redis CLI: ZCARD stm:velocity:merchant:m-vel-burst-01 → should equal number of runs
Redis CLI: ZRANGEBYSCORE stm:velocity:merchant:m-vel-burst-01 -inf +inf → all timestamps

test_cases.title: Negative feedback loop — mark analysis as incorrect
test_cases.description: Submit negative feedback (was_correct=false) on a previous analysis decision. Validates that feedback_notes with correction context are stored for future agent learning.
test_cases.precondition: Run TC-003 first; capture the alert_id from the response

json.feedback_notes: False positive: this was a legitimate micro-transaction from a known customer. Adjusting for future reference.

expected.sql_validation: SELECT was_correct, feedback_notes, feedback_at FROM analysis_decisions WHERE decision_id = '<alert_id>' → was_correct=false, feedback_notes contains 'False positive'
